headline: The man behind the legendary MPC, Roger Linn, stays focused with a single browser tab
summary: Roger Linn, creator of the iconic LM-1 and LinnDrum, is a pioneer in music technology. His innovative drum machines utilized samples and became instrumental on countless records. Despite his tech legacy, Linn maintains focus by strictly limiting himself to a single browser tab.

headline: Here are 38 Memorial Day deals we recommend for $50 or less
summary: Discover 38 fantastic Memorial Day tech deals, all priced at $50 or less. These affordable gadgets range from portable chargers to 4K streaming devices. Find great discounts without breaking the bank this holiday weekend.

headline: Hanging out in my favorite virtual coffee shop in Tokyo
summary: The author finds unexpected comfort and community in a virtual Tokyo coffee shop within a game. This digital space offers a unique sense of belonging, mirroring the appeal of a favorite physical cafe. It highlights the growing emotional connection users have with virtual environments.

headline: I have a new go-to browser
summary: The author reveals their new preferred web browser in this edition of Installer. The piece discusses personal experiences and top tech picks. The writer spent the week recovering from illness and rewatching beloved shows.

headline: Dead by Daylight Players React After 'Traumatizing' Cabin Stream Confirms Jason Voorhees as New Killer
summary: Dead by Daylight players finally saw Jason Voorhees confirmed as a new killer after a decade of anticipation. The announcement followed a 13-hour, 'traumatizing' livestream from Behaviour Interactive. Fans reacted strongly to the long-awaited reveal.

headline: Resident Evil Movie Director Says Fans Would Be 'Bummed' If He 'Just Did the Story of the Games'
summary: Resident Evil movie director Zach Cregger explains why his film won't strictly adapt the game's story. He believes a direct adaptation would ultimately disappoint fans. Cregger aims to offer a fresh, distinct narrative within the Resident Evil universe.

headline: Vampyros Lesbos/She Killed in Ecstasy Review
summary: This review covers two essential Jess Franco films: 'Vampyros Lesbos' and 'She Killed in Ecstasy.' Both cult classics have received a stunning 4K restoration treatment. It's a significant release for fans of European horror and exploitation cinema.

headline: The Mandalorian and Grogu: Did the New Republic Commit War Crimes?
summary: The new Star Wars movie, 'The Mandalorian and Grogu,' takes an unexpected narrative turn. The film poses the question of whether the New Republic might have committed a massive war crime. This controversial event occurs amidst an otherwise adventurous story.

headline: Iran 'getting a lot closer' to agreement with US, Trump says
summary: Former President Trump stated Iran is 'getting a lot closer' to an agreement with the US. Tehran also indicated progress in the ongoing talks. However, Iran clarified that nuclear weapons are not part of the initial framework discussions.

headline: France suspends extra EU border checks at Dover after hours-long queues
summary: France has temporarily suspended additional EU border checks at the port of Dover. The decision followed hours-long queues experienced by travelers due to the extra procedures. Soaring temperatures exacerbated the delays, prompting the suspension.

headline: Hottest day of year as 30.5C recorded and temperatures set to soar
summary: The UK recorded its hottest day of the year so far, with temperatures reaching 30.5C. Temperatures are forecasted to continue soaring across various regions. Amber heat health alerts are now active for the Midlands, eastern, and south-east England.

headline: 'Shameful' more spent on benefits than jobs for young people, says Milburn
summary: Alan Milburn criticized the 'shameful' disparity in spending on benefits versus job creation for young people. He advocates for urgent reforms to the welfare system. Milburn stresses the need to address the high numbers of young people not in work or education.
